title: iberis intermedia var. durandii
intro: Iberis intermedia var. durandii, also known as candytuft, is a perennial flowering plant in the mustard family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and grayish-green foliage. Traditionally, candytuft has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antitumor activities, which may help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Candytuft is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health, soothe digestive issues, and promote relaxation.
